title: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint - Devices And Vulnerability APIs
applies_to: Device inventory and vulnerability retrieval in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint

confirmed_facts.fact: Device inventory is exposed through the machines collection under the Defender for Endpoint API, with requests rooted at https://api.security.microsoft.com/api/machines.
The machines API supports OData operators such as $filter, $top, and $skip for inventory retrieval.

Vulnerability data can be retrieved as a machine-and-software view from the vulnerabilities-by-machines API, which supports OData filtering on fields such as cveId, machineId, productName, severity, and productVendor.
Software inventory details are available through software resources such as GET /api/Software/{Id}.
Security recommendation data is exposed through recommendation APIs under the same API family and is part of Defender for Endpoint threat and vulnerability management workflows.

implementation_notes: Treat machine inventory and vulnerability feeds as separate resources and join them by machine identifiers in your own pipeline.
Expect large result sets; design around server-side filtering and pagination rather than full dumps where possible.
For attack simulation data, use Microsoft Graph security attackSimulation endpoints rather than Defender for Endpoint APIs.
caveats: Permission scopes differ between machine, software, and recommendation endpoints.
Some vulnerability-management resources have plan or licensing constraints.
